paper_title: Physicochemical and Biological Assessment of Water Quality in the Tajuña River

methods.description: Process for measuring water velocity, temperature, conductivity, pH, and dissolved oxygen in situ, and collecting water samples for inorganic nutrient analysis.

steps.description: Water velocity, temperature, conductivity, pH, and dissolved oxygen were measured in situ according to standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water velocity, temperature, conductivity, pH, and dissolved oxygen in situ

steps.description: Water samples for inorganic nutrient analysis were collected using clean polyethylene containers, chilled to 1-4º C in the dark and transported to the laboratory within 24 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and transport water samples for nutrient analysis

steps.description: In the laboratory, water samples were filtered through pre-rinsed 0.45 µm cellulose acetate filters, and filtrates were used to determine concentrations of PO4-P, NH4-N, NO2-N and NO3-N by spectrophotometry.
steps.searchable_summary: filter water samples and determine nutrient concentrations by spectrophotometry

methods.description: Procedure for sampling epilithic diatoms from submerged stones, cleaning samples, and conducting taxonomic identification and density estimation.

steps.description: Epilithic diatoms were sampled in February 2006. At each sampling site, five submerged stones were removed from the riffle substratum, chilled to 1-4º C in the dark and transported to the laboratory within 24 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: collect submerged stones for diatom sampling

steps.description: The clean surface of each stone was separately scraped using a hard-bristled brush, the scraped material being collected in a small plastic tray with 50 ml of distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: scrape and collect diatom material from stones

steps.description: After homogenization, 5 ml aliquot of each sample was cleaned by oxidation with hot H2O2 to remove the organic matter, leaving only the diatom valves.
steps.searchable_summary: clean diatom samples by oxidation with hot H2O2

steps.description: After centrifugation, permanent slides were mounted using Naphrax (r.i.= 1.74) and a drop (1 µl) of cleaned samples.
steps.searchable_summary: mount diatom samples on slides using Naphrax

steps.description: Diatom density was estimated by counting the number of diatoms with a light microscope at 400× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate diatom density using light microscopy

steps.description: Taxonomic identification to species level was conducted following the Süßwasserflora von Mitteleuropa, using a light microscope at 1000× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: identify diatom species using light microscopy

methods.method_name: Water Quality Assessment Using Diatom Indices
methods.description: Application of Trophic Diatom Index (TDI) and Biological Diatom Index (IBD) to assess water quality based on diatom species composition and abundance.

steps.description: On the basis of species composition and abundance in the epilithic diatom community, we applied the Trophic Diatom Index (TDI) for assessing water quality.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Trophic Diatom Index to assess water quality

steps.description: The IBD is based on the tolerance of a relatively small list of about 210 diatom species to nutrients, and takes values between 1 and 20.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Biological Diatom Index to assess water quality

methods.description: Procedure for sampling aquatic macrophytes, estimating coverage percentage, and conducting taxonomic identification and water quality assessment using macrophyte indices.

steps.description: Aquatic macrophytes were sampled in June-July 2006. An area of about 100m² was selected at each sampling site, and coverage percentage was estimated for each taxon and for the whole community.
steps.searchable_summary: sample aquatic macrophytes and estimate coverage percentage

steps.description: Macrophyte samples were collected, chilled to 1-4º C in the dark, and transported to the laboratory for better taxonomic identification to species level.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and transport macrophyte samples for taxonomic identification

steps.description: On the basis of species composition and species abundance in the community of aquatic macrophytes, we applied the Index of Macrophytes (IM) and the Macroscopic Aquatic Vegetation Index to genera (IVAM-G) in order to assess water quality.
steps.searchable_summary: apply macrophyte indices to assess water quality
